title: Chapter Sixty-Three: The Seventh Apostle
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

He had overreached, and he knew it.

The sword song had wrung him dry. His body felt slack, waterlogged, as if someone had stuffed wet cloth into his chest. The upper dantian throbbed with a deep, nauseating fatigue that blurred the edges of his vision.

*This is hard.*

The two Blood Swordmasters did not wait for him to recover. Their arms blurred. Thunderclap bombs left their hands with the precision of hidden-weapon masters -- splitting the air, shrieking toward him.

He gripped the Desolate Sword's hilt.

Black hair flashed before his eyes.

For an instant he thought it was Myo-hwa. It was not. The energy was wrong -- opposite to the Zhongnan Sect's righteous waves. This was something darker. Older. Red sleeves fluttered with a strange nobility.

Two white hands moved. The thunderclap bombs changed direction as if plucked by wind -- Pear Blossom Grafting technique, executed at a supreme level, so fast there was no sound. The woman raised her palm skyward.

The bombs detonated in mid-air.

"Apostle," one of the swordmasters said, stepping back. No question in his voice. Only deference.

She turned.

One eye covered by a black patch. The other -- pomegranate red. Not painted. Not stained. The true colour of blood technique achievement made manifest in the iris itself.

She smiled.

"I felt it while suffering your dharma martial arts last time," she said. Her voice was silk over a razor's edge. "There was a raw feeling through energy sense. I thought it couldn't make sense. Then I saw the reports. Heard that strange sound."

Her lips curled.

"You were born with tremendous great master talent. To an unbelievable degree."

*Dangerous.*

His nape went cold. For the first time since entering the murim, Yeon-shin broke into cold sweat. She had deduced everything. The talent, the creation, the impossible speed of his growth. The Seventh Apostle was a supreme master who had faced Desolate Sect Squad Leaders -- her energy sense was refined beyond imagining.

And she wanted what lived inside his skull.

She walked toward him. Her energy waves spread in layers with each light footstep -- pressure building, air thickening. The momentum wrapping her body was terrifyingly cold.

No match possible. Not in this condition. Not even fresh. The Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm only worked when the gap was moderate. This was not moderate.

"Stubborn fools," she sneered, glancing past him. The Namgung family masters were only now beginning to stir. Too late. Far too late.

Blood Flame masters descended from the peak behind him, blocking the path between Yeon-shin and the Namgung family. Cutting him off.

"You're mine," the Apostle said.

*Karma.*

He had rushed in knowing Apostles would be here. He had trusted in his talent, in the Namgung family's reputation for sword and chivalry. Comradeship was good. Trust was excessive.

Her hand blurred.

His vision went black.

---

He woke to hunger so fierce it felt like his stomach was dissolving itself.

A ceiling he did not recognise. A face above him -- porcelain-white, framed by hair so black it looked like poured ink. One red eye. One black patch.

The Seventh Apostle wore a faint smile. Her hair brushed his cheek.

"You're the first Desolate Sect seed to set foot in our main hall. I should welcome you, shouldn't I?"

"Water," Yeon-shin said.

"What?"

"I'm thirsty."

His voice was a rasp. But he kept his eyes steady. They were fanatics. His life was in her hands already, and bowing to a fanatic only invited them to press harder.

The Apostle stared at him for a long moment.

Then she laughed -- a genuine, delighted sound that echoed wrong off the walls.

She leaned close. Her hair fell across his neck. The texture was creepily smooth. Her red eye filled his vision like a jewel held too near.

"You think I can't do anything to you?" she whispered. "I can make your eyeball like mine. Cut off your limbs. Give you our sect's regenerative ability. Your technique blocked my eye's recovery, but I don't have opposing martial arts like you. Everything else grows back."

Her breath, tinged with madness, brushed his nose.

"I'll wait forever while pulling out your fingers. Until you create martial arts for me."

He could not move. Paralysis points sealed. Only his mouth and his mind were free.

"Come back again," he said, keeping his voice flat. "Examine my Hundred Meetings point."

She was on him in an instant -- gentle wind, supreme-level body technique. Her fingertips pressed his crown. The point that even his uncle and grandfather had never dared to touch.

Her eye widened.

"You..."

"With your inner energy level, you can feel it with your hands. It is still growing. Even now."

"Such a constitution in the world..."

"Torture and heal me?" Yeon-shin asked with composure he did not feel. "In what lifetime?"

Something shifted behind her face. Not affection. Not mercy. Something worse.

Obsession.

The one-eyed demoness gazed down at him with the hunger of a collector who has found something that exists only once.

"You're mine," she breathed. "My possession. Create my martial arts. Be contained only in my eye. Until you burn up all your talent and fade away."

"Crazy woman."

The words came out before he could stop them. Her touch on his crown no longer felt gentle.

*Think. How do I survive this?*

His crown pulsed. The headache answered, constant and growing, and for once it felt like an ally.

---
